html:
Exclusive to:  

The Thermal Measurer is a Science Tab Structure. It is used to approximate the current ambient temperature. It can measure temperatures in a range from 0 to 35 degrees (actual temperature can go outside of this range). The gauge goes up as temperature increases (signifying Summer's approach) and goes down as the temperature falls (signifying Winter's approach). During the winter, it turns blue.

Downloadable Content

In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Thermal Measurer can be flooded. When flooded, it will visually emit sparks and cease to function.

Trivia

  • The reading from the Thermal Measurer goes down in the night due to the lack of heat from the sun. It goes back up in the daytime.
  • It was added in A Winter's Tale update.
  • Before the release of the Reign of Giants DLC, the Thermal Measurer was called the Winterometer.

Gallery

  • Summer.
  • Close to winter.
  • During mid-winter.
  • A burnt Thermal Measurer from the Reign of Giants DLC.
  • Thermal Measurer as seen in the poster for A Winter's Tale update.

Exclusive to:
“I am one heck of a scientist.”
–Wilson
“It would be better if it measured fire.”
–Willow
“Cold go up! Red go down!”
–Wolfgang
“Why do I feel that it is measuring my mortality?”
–Wendy
“HELLO, FRIEND”
–WX-78
“Measure before you act.”
–Wickerbottom
“We can build these half as tall back home.”
–Woodie
“Not the most useful invention, is it”
–Maxwell
“Amazing! Gold has mercurial properties here.”
–Wagstaff
“If önly it measured the heat öf battle.”
–Wigfrid
“How hot is it, Mr. Thermal Measurer?”
–Webber
“Why put numbers on what you can feel?”
–Walani
“Splendid. I should like to know when the worm is going to turn.”
–Warly
“Arrr brrrrrr!”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS HOT? 'TIS COLD?”
–Wilba
“Warm? Cold?”
–Wormwood
“Every aeronaut needs one in her possession.”
–Wheeler
“Assembling gadgets is so fulfilling.”
–Winona
“What do you say of the weather today?”
–Wortox
“Scale-less need this to tell if warm or cold?”
–Wurt
“I guess some people like to be more precise than "hot" and "cold".”
–Walter
“It might be prudent to keep an eye on the temperature.”
–Wanda
The Thermal Measurer is a Science Tab Structure. It is used to approximate the current ambient temperature. It can measure temperatures in a range from 0 to 35 degrees (actual temperature can go outside of this range). The gauge goes up as temperature increases (signifying Summer's approach) and goes down as the temperature falls (signifying Winter's approach). During the winter, it turns blue.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Thermal Measurer can be flooded. When flooded, it will visually emit sparks and cease to function.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The reading from the Thermal Measurer goes down in the night due to the lack of heat from the sun. It goes back up in the daytime.
It was added in A Winter's Tale update.
Before the release of the Reign of Giants DLC, the Thermal Measurer was called the Winterometer.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Máy đo nhiệt là một cấu trúc tab khoa học. Nó được sử dụng để ước tính nhiệt độ môi trường xung quanh hiện tại. Nó có thể đo nhiệt độ trong phạm vi từ 0 đến 35 độ (nhiệt độ thực tế có thể nằm ngoài phạm vi này). Đồng hồ đo tăng lên khi nhiệt độ tăng (biểu thị Mùa hè đang đến gần) và giảm xuống khi nhiệt độ giảm (biểu thị Mùa đông đang đến gần). Vào mùa đông, nó chuyển sang màu xanh.

The '''Thermal Measurer''' is a [[Science Tab]] [[Structure]]. It is used to approximate the current ambient temperature. It can measure temperatures in a range from 0 to 35 degrees (actual temperature can go outside of this range). The gauge goes up as temperature increases (signifying [[Summer]]'s approach) and goes down as the temperature falls (signifying [[Winter]]'s approach). During the winter, it turns blue.

== {{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content ==
In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, the Thermal Measurer can be [[Flooding|flooded]]. When flooded, it will visually emit sparks and cease to function.

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The reading from the Thermal Measurer goes down in the [[night]] due to the lack of heat from the sun. It goes back up in the [[day]]time.
* It was added in ''[[Version History#A Winter's Tale|A Winter's Tale]]'' update.
* Before the release of the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, the Thermal Measurer was called the Winterometer.
